section.p: # source_id: kpanlogo # title: Kpanlogo # source_url: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=query&prop=extracts&explaintext=1&format=json&titles=Kpanlogo # license: CC BY-SA 4.0 # attribution: Wik

figure.name: Ingested Figure

step.action: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form

figure.step: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form originating from the 1960s among urban youth in Accra, Ghana.

step.action: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dan

figure.step: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dance form, influenced by American rock and roll, and giving the younger Ga generations a point of distinction from their elders.

step.action: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the

figure.step: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the ground, with bent knees and bent back, and frequently features sexually suggestive motions.

step.action: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is dra

figure.step: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is drawn from older Ga drumming traditions, such as gome, oge and kolomashie.

step.action: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popu

figure.step: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popularized by the U.

step.action: rhythm and blues musician Bo Diddley.

figure.step: rhythm and blues musician Bo Diddley.

step.action: "Kpanlogo: A Detailed Description of One Arrange

figure.step: "Kpanlogo: A Detailed Description of One Arrangement of a West-African Music and Dance Genre.

step.action: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments a

figure.step: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments and detaining performers in its early days have been documented.

step.action: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments:

figure.step: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments: nono (metal bell), fao (gourd rattle), and kpanlogo drums.

step.action: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the mu

figure.step: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the music, supported by the fao.

step.action: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an

figure.step: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an ensemble, in the roles of "male voice", "female voice" and "master drum".

step.action: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most c

figure.step: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most common and oldest key patterns found in sub-Saharan Africa.

step.action: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as

figure.step: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as the son clave pattern heard in Cuban music and.

step.action: Bowling Green, Ohio: Bowling Green State Univers

figure.step: Bowling Green, Ohio: Bowling Green State University.
